Florida data center freeze: Pasco County joins regional moratoriums on tech growth
summary: Pasco County leaders enacted a one-year temporary ban on new data centers Tuesday to investigate how the massive computer facilities impact neighborhood resources and utility grids.

title: Pinellas schools consolidation: District seeks input on empty seats
summary: Pinellas County Schools is looking for community feedback as declining enrollment forces the district to plan another round of school consolidations. District leaders stress that these changes are necessary to keep local programs financially sustainable.

title: South Tampa road depression slows traffic
summary: Traffic is slowing near a busy South Tampa intersection after a possible road depression was reported.

title: US airstrikes hit northern Iran as it disables ship trying to run the blockade
summary: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United States intensified its strikes targeting Iran early Thursday, hitting targets further north as American forces also fired into a ship it accused of trying to break its naval blockade on the Islamic Republic. Iran retaliated with missile and drone fire targeting Bahrain and Kuwait before dawn. Days [...]

Fort Meade recall committee submits petitions to remove mayor, commissioners over data center approval
summary: A grassroots committee submitted formal recall petitions Wednesday to remove the Fort Meade mayor and two city commissioners following intense community backlash over a massive artificial intelligence data center project.

title: National Intelligence nominee Jay Clayton faces questions over potential election interference
summary: Democrats on the Senate Intelligence Committee are concerned Jay Clayton, nominated to be Director of National Intelligence, will use his role to undermine the security of U.S. elections.

title: Teen takeover or just hanging out? Bradenton police respond to 300 teens at Riverwalk
summary: 300 teens gathered at Bradenton Riverwalk Saturday. No arrests were made, but police say safety concerns remain. Experts explain the difference between a hangout and a takeover.

title: Elon Musk likely broke the law by promising voters $1 million payouts, Wisconsin board says
summary: Billionaire Elon Musk likely broke Wisconsin law when he promised to hand out $1 million checks to voters in the 2025 state Supreme Court election, a bipartisan panel has found.

title: Washing machine full of evidence: 19 plead guilty in Florida gang-affiliated defrauding scheme
